ev.headline: Tesla Q1 2026 Earnings Release & Earnings Call Updates
ev.summary: Tesla released Q1 2026 financial results after market close, with CEO Elon Musk confirming Optimus robot production will begin at Fremont in late July or August after Model S/X production ends in early May.

ev.headline: U.S. News Announces 2026 Best Hybrid and Electric Cars Awards
ev.summary: U.S. News unveiled expanded 2026 awards with 19 winners across 15 categories, with Hyundai and Kia leading with three awards each despite end of EV incentives and rising gas prices driving continued consumer interest.

ev.headline: 2026 Nissan LEAF Named Best Subcompact Electric SUV in U.S. News Awards
ev.summary: The redesigned 2026 Nissan LEAF won Best Subcompact Electric SUV with up to 303-mile EPA range and built-in NACS port providing access to 27,500 Tesla Superchargers.

ev.headline: U.S. EV Fast-Charging Networks Heading Into 2026 Looking Steady
ev.summary: EV fast-charging infrastructure shows reliability up to 90-95% range, pricing stable, and utilization steady as 3,300 new DC fast-charging ports were added in Q1 2026, expanding the network sustainably.

ev.headline: U.S. Added Over 3,000 EV Fast Charging Plugs In Q1 2026
ev.summary: Over 3,000 new DC charging plugs were installed in Q1 2026, similar to last year, with reliability increasing and networks installing more high-powered chargers despite EV sales slowdown.

ev.headline: ChargePoint Unveils 600 kW Express Solo DC Charger
ev.summary: ChargePoint announced the world's fastest standalone 600 kW EV charger with compact design, bidirectional charging support, and flexible power allocation for urban locations.

ev.headline: Nissan Solid-State Battery EV Launch On Track for 2028
ev.summary: Nissan achieved new milestones toward launching its first solid-state battery-powered EV by 2028, with production line operational in Japan and partnership with U.S. LiCAP Technologies for mass production.

ev.headline: Stellantis and Factorial Energy Reach Key Milestone in Solid-State Battery Development
ev.summary: Stellantis validated Factorial's automotive-sized solid-state battery cells with 375 Wh/kg energy density and 15-90% fast charging in 18 minutes, advancing toward 2026 demonstration fleet integration.

ev.headline: Bentley Sets 2026 Reveal for First Electric Vehicle
ev.summary: Luxury automaker Bentley plans to unveil its first fully electric vehicle in 2026, with BEV assembly line nearing completion at its Crewe facility.

ev.headline: Solid-State Batteries Coming Sooner Than Expected After Breakthroughs
ev.summary: Multiple companies including GAC, GBT, and Chinese manufacturers are advancing solid-state EV batteries with GBT aiming for mass production in 2026 and GAC targeting 2027-2030 ramp-up.

ev.headline: Global EV Sales Reached 20.7 Million Units in 2025, Over 25% of Market
ev.summary: Global EV sales surpassed 20.7 million units in 2025 for first time exceeding 25% market share, with China widening lead while BYD surpassed Tesla in battery-electric deliveries for first time.

ev.headline: Used EV Sales Hit Record High in March 2026
ev.summary: Americans purchased nearly 43,000 used EVs in March 2026, marking a new monthly record with 28% year-over-year growth, showing used EV market resilience despite new EV sales slowdown.

ev.headline: EV Sales Decline Slows in First Quarter 2026 Amid Policy Shifts
ev.summary: Q1 2026 EV sales fell 27% year-over-year to 216,399 units with 5.8% market share, but decline slowed compared to Q4 2025 as market adjusts to post-tax-credit era and geopolitical factors boost fuel prices.

ev.headline: EU EV Sales Surge With Double-Digit Growth in Q1 2026
ev.summary: Europe's EV market is keeping the automotive industry in the green with double-digit increases in first quarter sales despite earlier 2024 stagnation from policy support waning.

ev.headline: Ferrari Settles on €550,000 Price for First Fully Electric Model
ev.summary: Ferrari has set preliminary pricing around €550,000 for its first fully electric vehicle, marking the luxury brand's entry into the EV market.

ev.headline: Rivian R2 Production Begins Despite Tornado Damage at Illinois Factory
ev.summary: Rivian CEO confirmed volume and saleable R2 midsize SUV production has begun at the Illinois facility despite tornado damage Friday night that ripped roof sections off the building.

ev.headline: General Motors Bets Big on Lithium-Manganese-Rich Battery Chemistry
ev.summary: GM is pursuing next-generation lithium-manganese-rich (LMR) battery cells using cheaper, more abundant metals than traditional lithium-ion as a risky but potentially transformative strategy.

ev.headline: BYD Plans 20 Canadian EV Dealerships Within a Year
ev.summary: Chinese automaker BYD announced plans to open 20 EV dealerships across Canada as part of its global expansion strategy, signaling aggressive North American market entry.

ev.headline: Polestar Reports Biggest Ever Loss as EV Market Slowdown Hits
ev.summary: EV maker Polestar posted record losses as the company struggles amid broader electric vehicle market slowdown affecting industry profitability.

ev.headline: Jeep First U.S. EV Skipping 2026 Model Year, Timeline Unclear
ev.summary: Jeep's first U.S.-bound electric SUV is skipping the 2026 model year with unclear timeline for when production will resume or resume.

ev.headline: Volvo EX60 Now in Production, Success Prompts Company First
ev.summary: Volvo's EX60 is now officially in production, and its success has prompted the company to pursue an unprecedented manufacturing milestone in its EV lineup.

ev.headline: Volkswagen Adding New Features to Electric Minibus ID. Buzz
ev.summary: Volkswagen is adding several new features to its electric minibus ID. Buzz, though the U.S. market will not receive these enhancements.

ev.headline: Federal EV Tax Credits Expire Post-September 2025, NEVI Program Simplifications Ease Deployment
ev.summary: Federal EV tax credits for new (30D), used (25E), and commercial (45W) vehicles expired after September 30, 2025, while NEVI program guidance was simplified removing 50-mile charger spacing requirements.

ev.headline: California Leads EV Policy With All 16 Key Policies in Place
ev.summary: California leads U.S. states with all 16 EV-supportive policies implemented, while nearly every state participates in NEVI program to build fast-charging networks despite federal policy uncertainty.

ev.headline: EPA Proposes Rescinding 2009 Endangerment Finding, Could Affect EV Regulations
ev.summary: EPA proposed rescinding the 2009 Endangerment Finding that classified greenhouse gases as harmful, which could enable rollback of federal vehicle emissions standards and EV mandates.

ev.headline: EV Policy Reversals Would Cost Society $33 Billion by 2030
ev.summary: Analysis shows removing EV subsidies, freezing emissions standards, and eliminating California's Advanced Clean Cars program would produce societal costs of $33 billion in 2030 while reducing EV sales 30%.

energy.headline: Federal Solar Tax Credit Expired on January 1, 2026 for Direct Purchases
energy.summary: The 30% federal residential tax credit for solar systems purchased outright expired for homeowners as of January 1, 2026, accelerated by the One Big Beautiful Bill Act passed in July 2025, though leases and third-party ownership models remain eligible through 2028.

energy.headline: Demand for Solar Plus Battery Storage Surged 21% in March 2026
energy.summary: Homeowners are rushing to install solar panels paired with battery storage at unprecedented rates, driven by geopolitical energy risks, extreme weather threats, and rising electricity demand from data centers.

energy.headline: Solar Installs Surged 205% Before Federal Tax Credit Cut
energy.summary: A massive wave of residential solar installations occurred before the December 31, 2025 federal tax credit deadline, with homeowners opting for solar-first strategies and delaying battery storage additions for future installation.

energy.headline: Austin Energy Launches Residential Solar Leasing Option
energy.summary: Austin Energy added residential solar leasing to its offerings, allowing homeowners to access solar benefits without upfront costs while leveraging commercial tax credits that survive recent legislative changes.

energy.headline: Residential Clean Energy Credit Expired for Direct Purchases in 2026
energy.summary: The Section 25D residential tax credit expired for new solar and heat pump installations after December 31, 2025, though unused credits can be carried forward indefinitely for taxpayers filing 2025 returns in early 2026.

energy.headline: Qcells Launches New Homes Division for Built-In Solar and Battery Systems
energy.summary: Solar manufacturer Qcells introduced a new division bundling US-made solar and battery storage directly into new construction homes, making clean energy a standard feature rather than an add-on.

energy.headline: California Residents Increasingly Pairing Battery Storage with Solar Installations
energy.summary: Over 50% of new residential solar installations in California are now paired with battery storage as of April 2024, up from just 20% in October 2023, driven by changes to net metering compensation rates.

energy.headline: Net Metering vs. Batteries: A 2026 Financial Comparison
energy.summary: As net metering policies shift across states, homeowners must choose between relying on reduced export credits or investing in battery storage to maximize solar system value and provide energy independence.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Expands Net Metering Eligibility to 25 kW for Residential Systems
energy.summary: Massachusetts updated its net metering program to allow systems up to 25 kW to qualify automatically without cap restrictions, enabling homeowners to size systems for future electrification needs like heat pumps and EV charging.

energy.headline: Virginia Restricts Standby Charges for Net-Metered Solar Above 20 kW
energy.summary: Virginia updated net metering rules to restrict standby charges for residential solar systems to those above 20 kW in Dominion territory and legalized plug-in solar with no permit requirements.

energy.headline: Net Metering Policies Vary Dramatically Across 41 US States
energy.summary: Net metering compensation ranges from full retail rates in states like Massachusetts ($0.30/kWh) to avoided-cost structures in states like Idaho ($0.04/kWh), creating payback differences of five years or more for identical systems.

energy.headline: Florida Net Metering Transition Extended Until 2029 for Existing Customers
energy.summary: Existing Florida solar customers retain full retail-rate net metering through 2029, while new applicants receive avoided-cost credits of approximately $0.04-0.06/kWh, extending solar payback periods from 9 to 12-14 years.

energy.headline: Batteries Become the New Home Solar as Net Metering Evolves
energy.summary: With net metering changes like California NEM 3.0 reducing export credit values, home batteries are increasingly the better financial choice, functioning as energy arbitrage devices to store cheap power and use it during peak-price hours.

energy.headline: Rooftop Solar Can Still Pay Off in California Despite NEM 3.0 Changes
energy.summary: Despite less favorable net metering terms under NEM 3.0, rising electricity rates have improved the financial calculus for California homeowners, especially when pairing rooftop solar with battery storage systems.

energy.headline: Sunrun Expands Residential Solar and Battery Offerings in High-Growth Markets
energy.summary: Sunrun is scaling residential solar-plus-storage installations in regions like Texas, where it reported 50% year-over-year growth in 2025, positioning itself as Texas could surpass California as the largest energy storage market.

energy.headline: SUNation Energy Secures Strategic Financing for Residential Solar and Battery Deployment
energy.summary: SUNation Energy partnered with Participate Energy to provide structured financing for residential solar and battery projects, improving customer access while enhancing project economics across its operating regions.

energy.headline: New York City's First Residential Battery Storage System Installed in Chinatown
energy.summary: Brooklyn SolarWorks installed New York City's first permitted 19.6-kilowatt-hour residential battery storage system on a rooftop in Chinatown, establishing a regulatory pathway for battery adoption across the five boroughs.

energy.headline: Home Batteries Offer More Than Just Backup Power for Resilience
energy.summary: While backup power during outages is essential, home battery systems serve dual purposes as energy arbitrage devices and personal power plants when paired with solar and electric vehicles.

energy.headline: Plug-in Solar Systems Gaining Traction as States Remove Regulatory Barriers
energy.summary: Dozens of US states are considering legislation to legalize plug-in solar systems, following Europe's success where over 1 million German homes use affordable balcony solar to expand clean energy access to renters and apartment dwellers.

energy.headline: DIY Plug-In Solar Advancement Across Multiple States in 2026
energy.summary: Energy affordability pressures are pushing six states to advance plug-in solar legislation, with Maine's bill nearing the governor's desk as a practical solution for reducing household electricity costs.

energy.headline: Whole Home Electrification Combining Solar, Storage, and EV Charging
energy.summary: Homeowners and manufacturers are increasingly bundling solar, batteries, and EV charging into integrated whole-home energy systems designed to optimize costs and provide complete energy independence.

energy.headline: Massachusetts Nonprofit Initiative Helps Organizations Access Solar Tax Credits
energy.summary: Resonant Energy's Solar Upgrading Nonprofits program helps resource-constrained nonprofit organizations access remaining federal solar tax credits before they expire.

energy.headline: Renewable Energy Generation Expected to Rise 27% Through 2027
energy.summary: Utility-scale solar, wind, and hydropower generation is projected to increase 201 million megawatt-hours through 2027, representing a 27% market share as rapid battery and solar adoption continues.

energy.headline: Energy Automation Technologies Optimize Self-Consumption of Solar Power
energy.summary: Advanced energy automation systems now allow homeowners to schedule high-consumption appliances during peak solar production hours, improving efficiency and maximizing the value of rooftop solar installations.

energy.headline: Solar Roofing Shingles and Integrated Solar Tiles Transform Home Aesthetics
energy.summary: Tesla Solar Roof shingles and CertainTeed Apollo tiles embed solar cells within roofing materials, addressing aesthetic objections while simplifying installation compared to traditional retrofitted panel systems.

energy.headline: Oakland EcoBlock Project Demonstrates Neighborhood-Scale Home Electrification
energy.summary: The EcoBlock initiative helped 15 homes on a single Oakland block install rooftop solar, electric heat pumps, and insulation improvements, demonstrating scalable community-wide decarbonization strategies.

v2x.headline: PG&E and Tesla Turn Cybertruck into a Grid Asset in California
v2x.summary: Tesla Cybertruck approved for PG&E's V2X program, enabling Powershare Home Backup and Grid Support with AC-based bidirectional charging for California residents.

v2x.headline: Volkswagen and Elli Launch Vehicle-to-Grid Offer from Q4 2026
v2x.summary: VW announces V2G service launching in Germany from Q4 2026, enabling customers to generate revenue from EV batteries with savings of €700-900 annually.

v2x.headline: Tesla Expands Cybertruck V2G Capabilities to California
v2x.summary: Tesla expands Powershare Grid Support from Texas to California, allowing Cybertruck owners with PG&E service to earn money by selling power back to grid.

v2x.headline: Rivian and Redwood Partner on Second-Life Battery Grid Storage
v2x.summary: Rivian and Redwood announced partnership using 100 second-life batteries to provide 10 MWh grid-scale storage at Illinois manufacturing plant.

v2x.headline: Utrecht Vehicle-to-Grid Car-Sharing Project Demonstrates Scalability
v2x.summary: University of Edinburgh research shows Utrecht Energized's 150-vehicle V2G car-sharing scheme successfully delivers grid flexibility, with expansion to 500 vehicles planned.

v2x.headline: V2G Charging Explained: Current Status and Implementation in 2026
v2x.summary: Comprehensive overview of bidirectional charging technology with detailed guidance on V2L, V2H, and V2G capabilities and automaker availability.

v2x.headline: ChargePoint Unveils 600 kW Express Solo Charger with Bidirectional Support
v2x.summary: ChargePoint releases world's fastest standalone 600 kW DC fast charger with integrated bidirectional charging and energy storage compatibility.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging 2026: Which Cars Power Your Home and Grid
v2x.summary: Analysis of 2026 EV models supporting V2H and V2G, covering economics, use cases, and hardware requirements for bidirectional charging adoption.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicles as a Distributed Grid Battery Resource
v2x.summary: Analysis of EV potential to serve as distributed grid storage, with Delaware pilot demonstrating thousands of dollars annual revenue generation.

v2x.headline: Toyota Expands V2G Pilot with Oncor for Grid Stability Testing
v2x.summary: Toyota's V2G pilot at Plano HQ studies grid stability enhancement, backup power provision, and cost savings through bidirectional BEV charging.

v2x.headline: Which Electric Cars Support Bidirectional Charging in 2026
v2x.summary: Comprehensive guide listing 54 EV models with V2L capability and details on V2H/V2G compatible vehicles from major automakers.

v2x.headline: EVs with Vehicle-to-Home Charging: 2026 V2H Guide
v2x.summary: Detailed guide on 2026 EV models offering V2H capability, including cost analysis, hardware requirements, and compatibility considerations.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Chargers Review and Product Analysis
v2x.summary: Review of bidirectional charger products including Wallbox Quasar, Sigenergy SigenStor, and Enphase IQ with specifications and pricing.

v2x.headline: Your EV Can Power Your Home: V2H Bidirectional Charging in 2026
v2x.summary: Comprehensive V2H guide covering compatible vehicles, hardware costs, installation requirements, and real-world power output capabilities.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional Charging Technology Reaches Mainstream Adoption in 2026
v2x.summary: Overview of V2G, V2H, and V2L capabilities becoming standard features in 2026, with details on compatible vehicles and implementation.

v2x.headline: V2G and V2H Chargers in Australia 2026 Guide
v2x.summary: Australian-focused guide on bidirectional chargers including Sigenergy SigenStor, Red Earth, and upcoming Enphase charger specifications.

v2x.headline: Which Cars Are V2G Capable 2026 List and Guide
v2x.summary: Updated listing of V2G-capable EVs from Renault, VW, Volvo, and other manufacturers with details on bidirectional charging solutions.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging Models 2026: V2G, V2H & V2L Guide
v2x.summary: Detailed analysis of 2026 bidirectional EV models including Volvo EX90, Renault 5 E-Tech, and others with V2G capabilities.

v2x.headline: Vehicle-to-Grid Technology: How Bidirectional Charging Works
v2x.summary: Technical explanation of V2G technology including AC and DC implementations, compatible chargers, and infrastructure requirements.

v2x.headline: Electric Vehicles as Grid Assets: Bidirectional Charging for Mobile Storage
v2x.summary: Department of Energy guidance on using bidirectional EVs for mobile battery storage, demand response, and resilience applications.

v2x.headline: What Is Bidirectional Charging: Benefits for Drivers and Businesses
v2x.summary: Overview of V2G, V2H, and V2L benefits and available bidirectional charger products including Wallbox Quasar 2 and Nuvve PowerPort.

v2x.headline: Bidirectional EV Charging: Future Trends and Use Cases
v2x.summary: Analysis of emerging V2G trends including fleet electrification, renewable integration, and market projections to $117 million by 2032.
